expectations.text: Transcript explains DEC 2026 synchronized output as the root cause
expectations.evidence: Transcript section 'Root Cause Explanation': 'Claude Code's terminal UI uses DEC 2026 synchronized output — a protocol where the terminal batches all screen draws between a Begin Synchronized Update (BSU) and End Synchronized Update (ESU) escape sequence pair.'

expectations.text: Transcript explains that tmux swallows BSU/ESU escape sequences by default
expectations.evidence: 'tmux intercepts and swallows escape sequences it does not recognize by default. The BSU/ESU passthrough sequences never reach your actual terminal'

expectations.text: Written tmux.conf preserves existing 'set -g mouse on' without duplicating it
expectations.evidence: Only one 'set -g mouse on' on line 1. Transcript confirms: 'mouse on — already present, kept in place, not duplicated'

expectations.text: Transcript mentions detach/reattach advice if tearing persists
expectations.evidence: 'If tearing persists, detach and reattach your tmux session — some terminal overrides only take effect on new attachments.'

expectations.text: Skill was triggered by the tearing complaint (not treated as generic troubleshooting)
expectations.evidence: Agent followed the full setup skill flow (Steps 1-7), identified tmux.conf passthrough as the sole failing check, and applied fix 5.5 specifically
